findings.title: macOS defaults restore restarts UI services during dry-run and no-op restores
findings.suggested_fix: Generate a restored_any flag in the macOS defaults block, set it only after at least one defaults import is attempted or succeeds, and run killall cfprefsd/Finder/Dock/SystemUIServer only when DRY_RUN is not true and restored_any is true. Alternatively route the restarts through soft_run with the same DRY_RUN guard.

residual_risks: macOS defaults import remains inherently best-effort: some domains may be protected, ignored by current macOS versions, or require logout/reboot even when the command exits successfully.

Report-only captures for network, security, Time Machine, and printers suppress command errors, so missing system utilities or permission denials can produce sparse reports without failing generation.
testing_gaps: No regression appears to assert that generated install.sh --dry-run avoids killall/defaults side effects for macOS preferences.
No regression appears to cover a custom selection containing only default_apps, where the generated defaults directory exists but contains no plist imports.
